title: NOC 95101 – Labourers in metal fabrication | GTR Immigration
description: As Canada accelerates its industrial manufacturing and infrastructure projects in 2026, Labourers in Metal Fabrication (NOC 95101) form the essential support system for machine shops and structural metal plants. This role is a prime entry point for international workers looking to enter the Canadian "Trades" ecosystem. While classified under TEER 5, the high demand for material handlers and assistant fabricators in Ontario’s automotive and BC’s shipbuilding sectors provides a clear, documented pathway toward skilled machine operation and permanent residency.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Category5 (Occupations usually requiring short work demonstration)Major Group95 – Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilitiesSub-Major Group951 – Labourers in processing and manufacturing

duties: Assisting Skilled Trades: Helping Welders, Machinists, and Metal Fabricators by holding parts, positioning materials, and passing tools.
Material Handling: Loading and unloading metal stock and processed parts onto conveyors or worktables manually or using hoists.
Finishing Work: Grinding, bailing, and smoothing edges of metal parts using hand tools and portable grinders.
Maintenance: Cleaning work areas and equipment, and removing scrap metal and debris from the fabrication floor.
employment_requirements: Education: Some secondary school education is usually required; a high school diploma is preferred by most 2026 employers.
Training: Short-term on-the-job training is provided. No previous experience is mandatory, though it is considered an asset.
Physical Fitness: Ability to handle heavy metal components and stand for long durations in a busy industrial environment.
Safety: Mandatory 2026 WHMIS certification and site-specific training for overhead crane or forklift operation (if applicable).
job_outlook: The Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS) indicates that while automation is changing the fabrication floor, the need for human helpers to manage complex material logistics remains "Balanced." Retirement will account for nearly 75% of all job openings in this NOC over the next decade, ensuring a constant need for new international recruits.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook for fabrication labourers remains steady, with localized "High Demand" zones:
Ontario (GTA & Hamilton): Good prospects. Driven by the sustained growth of the motor vehicle parts manufacturing and structural metal industries.
British Columbia: Strong demand. Particularly in the Mainland and Vancouver Island regions to support federal shipbuilding contracts and transit infrastructure.
Manitoba & Saskatchewan: Stable demand. Focused on agricultural machinery manufacturing and heavy equipment fabrication.
Quebec: Moderate prospects. High concentration of opportunities in the Chaudière-Appalaches and Montérégie regions.
Why Work in Canada as a Fabrication Labourer?
Immediate Entry: Start your Canadian career without needing long-term academic credentials or prior certifications.
Skill Acquisition: Work alongside expert Welders and Machinists, gaining the hands-on experience needed to move into TEER 3 or 4 roles.
Path to PR: Eligible for specialized "In-Demand" provincial streams and the new 2026 federal "Construction & Trade" pilot programs.
Competitive Entry Wages: Manufacturing labourers in Canada often earn significantly higher than the national minimum wage due to shift premiums.
pathways: Ontario In-Demand Skills Stream: Fabrication labourers (NOC 95101) are explicitly eligible for provincial nomination in Ontario for those with a valid job offer.
TR to PR Pathway (April 2026): A landmark opportunity for 33,000 temporary residents to transition to PR. Labourers in "Essential Manufacturing" are high-priority candidates.
Saskatchewan Hard-to-Fill Skills Pilot: A streamlined pathway for semi-skilled workers in the manufacturing sector to obtain Permanent Residency.
BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled (ELSS): Provides a direct route for fabrication labourers working in specific regions of British Columbia.
